output:
You stand in a salt-streaked chapel, its altar rimed with dried brine; the air feels watched.
Ways out: n, s, e, w.

You stand in a long gallery lined with dust-caked ledger-desks; a sense of dread hangs thick. A clerk-thing shuffles among the shadows, its ledger-fingers ceaselessly counting.
Ways out: n, s, e, w.
The marks scratched into the salt-crusted surface resolve into fragments of an old ledger entry, though most of the sense has flaked away with the brine.

You stand in a cramped store-room, salt sacks slumped along the walls; everything is hushed and unmoving.
Ways out: n, s, e, w.

You close your hand around a cracked wax seal and tuck it away beneath your coat.
